title: rubus pubescens var. paracaulis
intro: Rubus pubescens var. paracaulis, also known as the American dewberry, is a shrub-like plant in the rose family that is native to North America. Its medicinal properties include its use as an astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial agent, making it useful in treating wounds, diarrhea, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Rubus pubescens var. paracaulis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in jams and jellies, allowing for various herbal preparations to be made from its berries and leaves.
